full_text:
State braces for storms and heat wave
Vijayawada: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) forecast two more days of fluctuating weather conditions across the state. APSDMA managing director Prakhar Jain stated that cloudy skies, thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, and scattered rainfall are expected to persist through Tuesday and Wednesday, while some districts may simultaneously experience intense heat.
According to APSDMA, a surface circulation is continuing over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal off the South Coastal Andhra region. Additionally, a trough extends from Rayalaseema across Tamil Nadu up to the Gulf of Mannar. Under the influence of these systems, light to moderate rains with thunderstorms are likely in several districts.
On Tuesday, districts including Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Visakhapatnam, Anakapalli, Kakinada, Konaseema, East Godavari, and West Godavari are expected to receive light to moderate rainfall with lightning in some areas. Meanwhile, Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla, Chittoor, and Tirupati districts may witness light showers at isolated places. APSDMA also cautioned that heat wave conditions may prevail in parts of Nellore district, particularly in Manubolu, Chillakur, and Gudur mandals, and in Tirupati district, including Buchinaidu Kandriga, Doravarisatram, Naidupeta, Ozili and Pellakur mandals on Wednesday.
Significant rainfall recorded till 5 p.m. on Monday included 57.5 mm at Lakshmipuram (East Godavari), 52.5 mm at Etcherla (Srikakulam), and 52.5 mm at Ramachandrapuram (Konaseema). Maximum temperatures soared above 40°C in several places with Anantapur recording 42°C. Authorities advised farmers and cattle rearers to avoid taking shelter under trees during thunderstorms and urged the public to take precautions against heat and humidity.
